# Contract: Sentinel grammar & zone framing (D1 / D2)

This is the authoritative byte-level contract for the generated zones. The
generator defines these as constants in ONE place; the template (FR-017) and the
inject-if-missing path (FR-008) reproduce this framing exactly so a template-born
map and an injection-migrated map are **byte-identical** (SC-001/SC-009).

## The six sentinel lines (exact bytes)

```text
<!-- GENERATED:INDEX:START (do not edit; regenerated by generate-spec-index.sh) -->
<!-- GENERATED:INDEX:END -->
<!-- GENERATED:PRS:START (do not edit; regenerated by generate-spec-index.sh) -->
<!-- GENERATED:PRS:END -->
<!-- GENERATED:BACKLINKS:START (do not edit; regenerated by generate-spec-index.sh) -->
<!-- GENERATED:BACKLINKS:END -->
```

- HTML comments — invisible in rendered Markdown, greppable, independently
  positionable.
- The `START` line carries the `(do not edit…)` note; the `END` line is bare.
- Sentinels are matched by **full-line string equality** against these constants,
  never by a loose regex over substrings, so a `GENERATED:` token appearing in
  prose can never be mistaken for a sentinel.

## Fixed zone order

`INDEX` → `PRS` → `BACKLINKS`. This order is identical in the template and the
injector.

## Byte framing rules (the determinism trap — pin exactly)

1. Exactly **one blank line** separates the end of the existing map-note body
   from the first sentinel (`GENERATED:INDEX:START`).
2. Each zone is: the `START` line, then the rendered body (or nothing if empty),
   then the `END` line — each on its own line.
3. Exactly **one blank line** separates one zone's `END` line from the next zone's
   `START` line.
4. The file ends with the `GENERATED:BACKLINKS:END` line followed by a **single
   trailing newline** (`\n`), with no extra trailing blank line.
5. An **empty zone** is the `START` and `END` lines on **consecutive lines with no
   blank line and no body between them**. Empty therefore means **link-free**,
   which keeps PRSG-002's stale-index lint green (it greps `[]()` links; the
   sentinels contain none).

## Anchor

The three-zone block is appended at the **end of the map-note body**, immediately
after the existing introductory paragraph (spec.md Assumptions default). The
injector inserts the block at this same anchor; it never edits frontmatter or the
`up:` value.

## Worked example (an in-scope spec with an empty INDEX, empty PRS, and two BACKLINKS rows)

```text
# PRSG-003 — Map of Content

Navigation map for this spec. The `up:` link above points to the parent technical
roadmap. ...intro paragraph...

<!-- GENERATED:INDEX:START (do not edit; regenerated by generate-spec-index.sh) -->
<!-- GENERATED:INDEX:END -->

<!-- GENERATED:PRS:START (do not edit; regenerated by generate-spec-index.sh) -->
<!-- GENERATED:PRS:END -->

<!-- GENERATED:BACKLINKS:START (do not edit; regenerated by generate-spec-index.sh) -->
- [spec.md](spec.md)
- [plan.md](plan.md)
<!-- GENERATED:BACKLINKS:END -->
```

(The INDEX and PRS zones are empty/link-free here — INDEX is dormant pending
PRSG-004; PRS is empty pending a `.process/prs.json`. The two BACKLINKS rows are
relative to the MOC's own directory and resolve under `specs/prsg-003-spec-index/`.)
